title: Vincetoxicum tenue
intro: Vincetoxicum tenue, also known as the slender Vincetoxicum or Higo-kin-bai-ge, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Vincetoxicum tenue is used in medicine due to its reported health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include vincamine, periwinkle alkaloids, and other apocynin derivatives, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vincetoxicum tenue is often prepared as a dried root or made into a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat a range of ailments.
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum tenue is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to certain regions in Asia and is part of the Asclepiadaceae or dogbane family, which is now often considered a subfamily (Asclepiadoideae) within the Apocynaceae family. It belongs to the genus Vincetoxicum, which includes various species of perennials and annuals. Vincetoxicum tenue is often found growing in woodland areas and is characterized by its delicate, pale yellow flowers. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Apocynaceae family.
